person.name: Hristo Hristov

He specializes in designing robust multi-tenant Kubernetes architectures and implementing policy-driven governance. Hristo also contributes to open-source initiatives as a maintainer for the CNCF's Capsule project, emphasizing multi-tenancy and policy-driven solutions.

person.name: Rafal Zdziech
person.bio: Rafal Zdziech is a Senior Platform Engineer with deep expertise in cloud-native infrastructure, DevOps, and automation. He specializes in Kubernetes, CI/CD, and infrastructure as code, helping teams build scalable and reliable platforms.

person.name: Rodolfo Rivera López
person.bio: From the very moment I installed Red Hat 9 (without the Enterprise Edition), I knew open source was my calling. The challenge, the deep-seated need for knowledge to understand, the pure satisfaction of making something work, and then the drive to achieve much more—these forged my vision of technology. For me, every technical challenge is a designed opportunity to learn, execute, and iterate (not always in that order), a cycle that has given me powerful tools to deconstruct complex systems. This is my proven methodology for mastering what fascinates me most, a journey that has taken me through realms like GNU/Linux and BSD system administration, fullstack development, DevOps/Automation, Security and ultimately, the Kubernetes universe.  I believe technology defines us. Therefore, creating and applying it in the best possible way is, at its core, a contribution to a better society. This is why my commitment to learning is absolute and has no expiration date. It is the foundation from which I strive to reach the giants who built this wonderful open-source world, sharing whatever I can in return. Collaboration has proven to be the most viable path to the unprecedented; now is the time for all of us to contribute.

person.name: Roby Chen
person.bio: I am honored to continue serving in this role. Over the past few years, My company DaoCloud has made continuous contributions to the open-source community. Our contributions to Kubernetes, a core cloud-native project, have ranked among the top three globally over the past year. In 2022, we will also take on a contributing seat on Istio's steering committee. We have independently open-sourced nine projects, including Clusterpedia, Merbridge and Piraeus which have successfully entered CNCF Sandbox incubation. DaoCloud continues to contribute significantly towards cloud-native technology and improve CNCF's technical ecosystem.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Firstly, I hope that cloud-native technology will remain highly connected to China, and that the integration of the technology world will not be affected by geography, politics, international conflicts and other factors. Secondly, I hope that cloud-native technology can break through the barriers between technology and users, and that more end-user voices can be fed back to CNCF. Thirdly, I hope to promote the building of cloud-native technology talent and further improve the cloud-native education system, so that CNCF will become a sustainable organization.
person.company: SHANGHAI DAOCLOUD NETWORK TECHNOLOGY CO. LTD. (DAOCLOUD)

This role provides a unique platform to engage with a diverse and talented community, enabling the exchange of knowledge and ideas that drive innovation. It is an honor to serve as a bridge between the CNCF and the broader community, fostering collaboration and mutual support.
